skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with 40+ models including Google Veo, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse, Wan, and Grok via inference.sh CLI. Supports text-to-video, image-to-video, video editing, AI avatars, lipsync, and video upscaling.
skill.summary: Generate AI videos with 40+ models via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill providing guidance on using the inference.sh CLI for AI video generation. All static findings are false positives: bash commands are in markdown code blocks (not executed), URLs point to legitimate inference.sh infrastructure, and no cryptographic algorithms are present. This skill contains no executable code and poses no security risk.

content.value_statement: Creating professional videos with AI requires exploring multiple services and APIs. This skill provides commands to generate videos using 40+ AI models including Google Veo, Seedance 2.0, and HappyHorse through a unified CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text prompts using Google Veo, Grok, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse and other models
Animate static images into videos with Wan 2.5, HappyHorse I2V, and Seedance 2 I2V
Create AI avatars and talking heads with OmniHuman and Fabric lipsync
Edit existing videos using natural language instructions with HappyHorse Edit
Upscale video quality and add sound effects (Foley) to videos
Generate synchronized audio tracks for videos with compatible models
limitations: Requires an inference.sh account and CLI installation before use
Video generation occurs on remote servers, not locally
Model availability and quotas depend on inference.sh service
No local video processing or editing capabilities

use_cases.title: Content Creator Workflow
use_cases.description: Generate social media videos quickly with AI. Create product demos, explainer videos, and marketing content by simply describing what you want.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Developer Integration
use_cases.description: Build video generation into your applications using the belt CLI. Automate video creation for user-generated content platforms.

use_cases.title: Video Production Aid
use_cases.description: Generate reference footage, B-roll, and visual elements for video production projects. Create AI avatars for narrations and presentations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the belt CLI to generate a 5-second video of [your scene description] using Veo 3.1 Fast
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick video generation from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Image Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the Wan 2.5 image-to-video model to animate this image: [image URL]. Prompt: [animation description]
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert a static image into a moving video

prompt_templates.title: AI Avatar with Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking head video using OmniHuman 1.5 with [portrait image] and [audio file URL]
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate an AI avatar with synchronized speech

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Video with Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 10-second video with Seedance 2 T2V. Include audio generation. Prompt: [detailed scene description]
prompt_templates.scenario: Professional video with music and sound effects

output_examples.input: Generate a drone shot flying over a forest
output_examples.output: A 5-second video file saved locally showing aerial footage of a forest canopy with natural camera movement

output_examples.input: Animate a portrait image with a speech audio file
output_examples.output: A talking head video where the person in the image speaks with realistic lip synchronization

best_practices: Use specific, detailed prompts describing camera movement, lighting, and subject actions for better results
Start with fast models like Veo 3.1 Fast for quick iteration, then use quality models for final outputs
Test with short durations first to verify the output matches your vision before generating longer videos
anti_patterns: Avoid vague prompts like 'make a cool video' - include specific actions, camera moves, and visual details
Do not expect real-time generation - video creation takes time even with fast models
Avoid using copyrighted characters or faces without proper licensing in your prompts

faq.question: What AI models are available?
faq.answer: 40+ models including Google Veo 3.1, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse 1.0, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, and Fabric for various video generation tasks.

faq.question: How do I install the required CLI?
Can I generate videos with audio?
faq.answer: Yes. Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Grok Video, and Seedance 2 models support audio generation. Use the generate_audio parameter.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Fast models complete in under a minute. Higher quality models may take several minutes. Check status with belt app status.

faq.question: What image formats are supported for I2V?
faq.answer: Standard formats like JPG, PNG, and WebP are supported. High-resolution images (1080P+) work best with HappyHorse.

faq.question: Can I edit existing videos?
faq.answer: Yes. HappyHorse Edit allows natural language video editing: change backgrounds, extend scenes, or modify elements.
